metadata.title: The Last Testimony
metadata.author: AdventureKit Studio
metadata.summary: A noir legal thriller vertical slice about a defense attorney proving that the witness saw an impossible open window.

storyBible.premise: At dawn, defense attorney Mara Vey must find the contradiction hidden in a final testimony before an innocent man is convicted.
storyBible.tone: noir legal thriller, elegant, tense, intelligent
storyBible.targetPlayStyle: dialogue, evidence inspection, contradiction presentation, branching pressure
storyBible.visualDirection: rain-streaked windows, polished wood, cold blue dawn, amber legal lamps
storyBible.coreMystery: A window that should have been closed was described as open.
storyBible.writingStyleGuide: Precise legal lines, noir narration, emotional restraint until evidence lands.

characters.biography: A defense attorney who hears the one impossible word in a polished statement.
characters.secrets: Her first mentor hid evidence to win.
characters.motivations: Save Elias and keep the court honest.
characters.visualDescription: Dark suit, rain-damp collar, file held like a blade.
characters.baseImagePrompt: Noir defense attorney in predawn courtroom, cold rain light, cinematic portrait.
characters.relationshipNotes: Respected by Judge Hale; opposed by Cassian.

variants.imagePrompt: Defense attorney presenting evidence in noir courtroom, rainlit windows.

characters.biography: A night porter who saw too much or was told what to see.
characters.secrets: She was coached after seeing only lightning and glass.
characters.motivations: Survive the courtroom without admitting fear.
characters.visualDescription: Pale coat, wet cuffs, fingers tight on witness rail.
characters.baseImagePrompt: Exhausted witness at courtroom stand, rain window behind, noir lighting.
characters.voiceNotes: Repeats lines until pressure cracks them.

variants.description: Lyra realizes the testimony cannot stand.
variants.imagePrompt: Shocked courtroom witness recoiling, rain-streaked window, noir legal thriller.

characters.biography: A judge with little patience for drama and deep respect for proof.
characters.secrets: He suspects the trial moved too quickly.
characters.visualDescription: Older judge under high bench shadow, silver hair, folded hands.
characters.baseImagePrompt: Severe judge in shadowed predawn courtroom, cinematic noir portrait.
characters.relationshipNotes: Gives Mara one chance at a time.

variants.description: The judge recognizes a real contradiction.
variants.imagePrompt: Judge leaning forward in noir courtroom, alert expression, rain windows.

characters.biography: A prosecutor who wins by making uncertainty feel vulgar.
characters.secrets: He knows the window photo arrived late.
characters.motivations: Protect the verdict and his record.
characters.visualDescription: Tailored charcoal suit, immaculate tie, half-lit smile.
characters.baseImagePrompt: Confident prosecutor in noir courtroom, cold dawn light, cinematic portrait.

variants.description: Cassian counters after the window reveal.
variants.imagePrompt: Irritated prosecutor stepping forward in rainlit courtroom.

characters.biography: A clerk accused of killing the councilman who was about to expose a scheme.
characters.secrets: He hid a ledger to protect someone else.
characters.motivations: Survive the verdict without betraying the real witness.
characters.visualDescription: Thin man at defense table, cuffed hands still.
characters.baseImagePrompt: Accused man at defense table in predawn noir courtroom, solemn.

variants.description: Elias hears the first crack in the case.
variants.imagePrompt: Accused man standing in noir courtroom, dawn light, restrained relief.

characters.biography: The clerk who can prove the evidence photo arrived after testimony was drafted.
characters.secrets: He copied the receipt before Cassian's aide collected it.
characters.motivations: Tell the truth without losing his post.
characters.visualDescription: Raincoat over courthouse uniform, ink-stained fingers.
characters.baseImagePrompt: Nervous courthouse records clerk in dim archive, noir rainlight.

variants.imagePrompt: Courthouse clerk holding logbook in dim records room, noir style.

items.name: Elevator Keycard
items.description: A keycard showing basement elevator access at 11:42 p.m.

items.name: Torn Security Schedule
items.description: A ripped schedule that contradicts the guard's claimed post.

items.name: Raincoat Receipt
items.description: A kiosk receipt timestamped minutes before the alarm.

items.name: Crime Scene Photo - Study Window
items.description: The latch is closed, but a narrow stain sits beneath the sill.

items.name: Weather Report - 01:10 AM
items.description: Heavy rain and eastern winds near the courthouse district.

assets.title: Predawn Courtroom
assets.description: Courtroom before dawn under rain and fluorescent hum.
assets.prompt: Cinematic noir courtroom before dawn, rain streaking tall windows, judge bench in shadow, witness stand visible, 16:9, no text.

assets.title: Witness Stand Rain Window
assets.description: Close witness view with rain window behind.
assets.prompt: Cinematic close view of courtroom witness stand, rain-streaked window behind, cold dawn light, premium visual novel style, 16:9, no text.

assets.prompt: Courthouse basement records room, one glowing terminal, shelves of files, noir rain ambience, 16:9, no text.

assets.prompt: Narrow courthouse security desk with umbrellas and thermal paper, noir night rain, 16:9, no text.

assets.description: Crime scene window photo with latch detail.
assets.prompt: Fictional crime scene study window photo, closed latch, narrow water stain beneath sill, no text, no gore.

assets.title: Weather Report
assets.prompt: Fictional city weather report prop, storm symbols, no real agency, no readable personal data.

assets.title: Elevator Keycard
assets.prompt: Fictional courthouse elevator keycard evidence, generic symbols, no logo.

assets.title: Torn Security Schedule
assets.prompt: Torn fictional courthouse security schedule, rain stain, no readable real names.

assets.title: Raincoat Receipt
assets.prompt: Fictional damp kiosk receipt, timestamp emphasis, no real business.

gameplayHooks.title: Present the Contradiction
gameplayHooks.narrativePurpose: Mara must prove the witness's final answer cannot be true.
gameplayHooks.expectedPlayerAction: Select the basement denial and present the elevator keycard.
gameplayHooks.uiLayoutNotes: Statement cards center, evidence tray right.
gameplayHooks.mobileGestureNotes: Tap statement, tap evidence, press present.
gameplayHooks.agentPrompt: Design contradiction gameplay for an impossible basement elevator denial.

gameplayHooks.title: The Window That Was Closed
gameplayHooks.narrativePurpose: The player identifies the exact impossible line.
gameplayHooks.expectedPlayerAction: Select statement four and present the window photo.

gameplayHooks.title: The Stain Beneath the Sill
gameplayHooks.narrativePurpose: Let the player identify the staged-window detail.
gameplayHooks.expectedPlayerAction: Tap the narrow stain below the latch.
gameplayHooks.uiLayoutNotes: Photo zoom card with focus box.
gameplayHooks.agentPrompt: Create an inspection hook spec for a window stain.

gameplayHooks.title: Elevator Log Parse
gameplayHooks.narrativePurpose: Find the impossible basement access timestamp.
gameplayHooks.gameplayPurpose: Inspect a log and select the matching keycard row.
gameplayHooks.agentPrompt: Design a document row inspection hook.

gameplayHooks.title: One More Question
gameplayHooks.narrativePurpose: Mara must choose pressure or patience under judge scrutiny.
gameplayHooks.agentPrompt: Design a courtroom timed pressure hook.

gameplayHooks.title: Receipt Timeline
gameplayHooks.narrativePurpose: Build the sequence between kiosk, basement, and alarm.

gameplayHooks.title: Who Coached Lyra
gameplayHooks.narrativePurpose: Connect Lyra's phrasing to Cassian's aide.

gameplayHooks.title: Closing Argument Builder
gameplayHooks.narrativePurpose: Convert discoveries into the final pitch.
gameplayHooks.agentPrompt: Design a closing argument builder hook.

scenes.title: Rain at the Courthouse
scenes.purpose: Set noir pressure and start investigation.
scenes.synopsis: Mara arrives before dawn with one final testimony to break.
scenes.camera: wide courthouse steps into courtroom dissolve
scenes.background: Stone steps reflecting blue security lights and late rain.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Rainy courthouse steps before dawn, blue security lights, noir legal thriller, 16:9, no text.

layoutNotes.mobileLandscape: 16:9 courtroom stage with case title top left.

blocks.text: By morning, the trial has one witness left and one truth too many.

blocks.speaker: Mara Vey
blocks.text: We do not need the whole night. We need the one minute the witness keeps stepping around.

scenes.title: The Verdict Before Dawn
scenes.purpose: Cinematic intro at the brink of conviction.
scenes.synopsis: Mara asks the court for one more question.
scenes.camera: judge centered, witness upper right, defense lower left
scenes.background: The courtroom waits in blue predawn rain.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Cinematic noir courtroom before dawn with rain on tall windows, judge bench shadow, 16:9, no text.

layoutNotes.mobileLandscape: Dialogue bottom 28%, evidence tray disabled right.

blocks.speaker: Judge Arden Hale
blocks.text: Counselor Vey. This court has granted you every possible delay.

blocks.speaker: Mara Vey
blocks.text: Then grant me one more question.

scenes.title: Basement Log
scenes.purpose: Give the keycard clue and introduce document inspection.
scenes.synopsis: Tobin shows the elevator access record.
scenes.camera: terminal glow on logbook and clerk hands
scenes.background: A humming records room with a single terminal awake in the dark.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Courthouse basement records room, glowing terminal, shelves, noir rain atmosphere, 16:9, no text.

blocks.speaker: Tobin Vale
blocks.text: Nobody was meant to use the basement elevator after eleven.

choices.label: Return to court with the keycard

scenes.title: Records Crosscheck
scenes.purpose: Confirm the basement timestamp against the witness route.
scenes.synopsis: Mara matches the keycard row to a door camera time nobody mentioned.
scenes.background: The archive terminal throws blue light across a logbook and a wet sleeve.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Courthouse records room terminal and logbook under blue light, raincoat sleeve visible, noir investigation, 16:9, no text.

layoutNotes.mobileLandscape: Document row comparison center with evidence tray right.

blocks.speaker: Mara Vey
blocks.text: The elevator log does not place Lyra near the crime. It places her near the story she was told to repeat.

blocks.speaker: Tobin Vale
blocks.text: And the camera receipt came in after the statement was drafted.

choices.label: Return to court with the cross-check

scenes.title: Security Desk
scenes.purpose: Add schedule contradiction and route to receipt clue.
scenes.synopsis: A torn schedule puts the witness near the wrong door.
scenes.background: A narrow desk stacked with umbrellas, thermal paper, and a silent radio.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Narrow courthouse security desk with umbrellas and paper, noir rain night, 16:9, no text.

layoutNotes.mobileLandscape: Evidence cards on right, dialogue bottom.

blocks.speaker: Tobin Vale
blocks.text: The guard swapped posts when the rain started. Said the east doors were leaking again.

scenes.title: Kiosk Receipt
scenes.purpose: Add timestamp evidence for the final court sequence.
scenes.synopsis: A damp receipt proves someone stayed later than claimed.
scenes.background: A closed coffee kiosk beneath a maintenance light that buzzes like a held breath.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Closed courthouse kiosk, damp receipt under maintenance light, noir legal thriller, 16:9, no text.

layoutNotes.mobileLandscape: Receipt card center, timeline prompt ready.

blocks.text: The receipt is small, damp, and decisive: a raincoat bought after the witness claimed she was already home.

scenes.title: Final Testimony Review
scenes.purpose: Present the statement-card structure and contradiction target.
scenes.synopsis: Lyra repeats the line about the open study window.
scenes.camera: witness left, statements center, evidence tray right
scenes.background: The witness stand sits under cold dawn light with rain behind it.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Courtroom witness stand close view, rain-streaked window behind witness, premium visual novel noir, 16:9, no text.

layoutNotes.mobileLandscape: Witness 25%, testimony cards center, evidence tray 25%.

blocks.speaker: Lyra Mont
blocks.text: The study window was open. I saw Elias Vorn standing inside, holding the letter opener.

choices.label: Press the statement in open court

scenes.title: Witness Stand
scenes.synopsis: Mara challenges Lyra's impossible open-window account.
scenes.camera: witness upper right, Mara lower left, judge centered
scenes.background: A courtroom washed in gray daylight, every surface too polished for comfort.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Noir courtroom witness stand in gray dawn, rain windows, polished wood, 16:9, no text.

layoutNotes.mobileLandscape: Hook card over testimony cards, evidence rail visible.

blocks.speaker: Mara Vey
blocks.text: The court record says otherwise. Let's test the exact minute you say never happened.

blocks.speaker: Lyra Mont
blocks.text: The elevator log was not my business. I stayed where visitors could see me.

blocks.speaker: Lyra Mont
blocks.text: I left through the front doors before the alarm. I never went below the lobby.

scenes.title: Objection Sustained
scenes.synopsis: The judge punishes speculation but Mara can recover with stronger proof.
scenes.background: The courtroom sound drops away after the gavel.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Noir courtroom gavel foreground, defense attorney in shadow, rain windows, 16:9.

layoutNotes.mobileLandscape: Setback text center with recovery choices.

blocks.speaker: Judge Arden Hale
blocks.text: The court will not follow speculation into the basement.

scenes.title: Witness Cracks
scenes.purpose: Reward evidence presentation and open the staged-window reveal.
scenes.synopsis: Lyra admits she knew something before she could have known it.
scenes.camera: bead of rain on witness sleeve, close
scenes.background: A bead of rainwater falls from the witness's sleeve onto the rail.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Courtroom witness closeup, rainwater on sleeve, shocked expression, noir legal thriller, 16:9.

layoutNotes.mobileLandscape: Witness portrait right, revelation copy left.

blocks.speaker: Lyra Mont
blocks.text: I only went down because I heard the alarm before it sounded upstairs.
The contradiction is not just where she was. It is what she knew too early.

scenes.title: Rain on the Inside
scenes.purpose: Major game moment from the reference scene.
scenes.synopsis: Mara proves the open window was staged after the murder.
scenes.camera: projected photo center, Mara left, Lyra right
scenes.background: The crime scene photograph expands over the courtroom wall.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Dramatic courtroom evidence reveal with projected crime scene window photo, highlighted stain, noir, 16:9, no text.

layoutNotes.mobileLandscape: Photo dominates center with highlighted detail.

blocks.speaker: Mara Vey
blocks.text: If that window had been open, the rain would have crossed the sill and soaked the study floor.

choices.label: Pressure Lyra about who coached her

scenes.title: Judge's Warning
scenes.synopsis: Mara is warned to present proof, not instinct.
scenes.background: Judge Hale leans into a silence that feels final.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Severe judge leaning forward in noir courtroom, rain windows, 16:9.

layoutNotes.mobileLandscape: Judge portrait center with recovery choice.

blocks.speaker: Judge Arden Hale
blocks.text: One more unsupported accusation and this court is finished listening.

scenes.title: Staged Window Path
scenes.synopsis: The staged window points to someone inside the prosecution timeline.
scenes.camera: ledger and photo side by side
scenes.background: The photo and logbook sit together like they were waiting to meet.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Noir records table with crime scene photo and logbook, rain ambience, 16:9.

layoutNotes.mobileLandscape: Evidence pair center, branch choices bottom.

blocks.speaker: Mara Vey
blocks.text: The killer did not need a witness. They needed a witness who believed herself.

scenes.title: Mara Explains the Photo
scenes.purpose: Inspection failure branch without blocking story.
scenes.synopsis: Mara explains the stain herself, but loses momentum.
scenes.background: The focus box finally finds the narrow stain.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Courtroom projection with late evidence highlight, noir rain, 16:9.

blocks.text: The court understands the stain, but not before the moment cools.

scenes.title: Lyra's First Crack
scenes.synopsis: Lyra admits someone gave her the phrasing.
scenes.camera: Lyra tight closeup, Cassian entering frame
scenes.background: Lyra's hands leave marks on the witness rail.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Witness breakdown in noir courtroom, hands tense on rail, prosecutor shadow, 16:9.

blocks.speaker: Lyra Mont
blocks.text: He said if I used the exact words, nobody would ask about the rain.

scenes.title: Prosecutor Counterattack
scenes.purpose: Reckless branch where Cassian strikes back.
scenes.synopsis: Cassian turns Mara's accusation into a threat to the defense.
scenes.background: Cassian steps into the aisle before the judge can speak.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Irritated prosecutor stepping into noir courtroom aisle, rain windows, 16:9.

layoutNotes.mobileLandscape: Cassian portrait right, defensive choices bottom.

blocks.speaker: Cassian Roe
blocks.text: The defense is not revealing truth. It is manufacturing fog.

scenes.title: Closing Argument
scenes.synopsis: Mara chooses the proof order that will define the verdict.
scenes.background: The courtroom has become still enough to hear rain choose a window.
scenes.backgroundPrompt: Defense attorney closing argument in predawn noir courtroom, rain windows, cinematic 16:9.

endings.title: Timeline Revealed
endings.summary: The witness admits she knew about the alarm before it sounded, and the court sees the staged window for what it was.
endings.cinematicNotes: Rain brightens as the judge calls for a recess.

endings.title: Thin Acquittal
endings.summary: Mara creates enough doubt to save Elias, but the person who staged the window remains unnamed.
endings.cinematicNotes: Elias exhales while Cassian gathers untouched papers.

endings.title: Case Reopened
endings.summary: The verdict is suspended and the court orders a new investigation into the staged evidence.
endings.cinematicNotes: The projector keeps glowing after everyone stands.
